meta.description: Country citizenship routes compiled from a canonical D1 release plus the read-only legacy remainder.

acquisition_modes.ancestry: Citizenship through a parent, grandparent, wider descent rule, restoration, or documented heritage connection.
acquisition_modes.naturalization: Citizenship after residence or another qualifying domestic status.
acquisition_modes.birth: Citizenship or an accelerated route triggered by place of birth.
acquisition_modes.investment: Direct investor citizenship; residence-by-investment is not classified as citizenship by investment.

coverage_states.reviewed: The recorded finding has been checked against an official source.
coverage_states.partial: At least one rule is recorded, but this mode is not yet exhaustive.
coverage_states.pending: A credible legal basis exists but current operation is not verified.
coverage_states.unchecked: No route-level review has been completed for this mode.

routes.title: Citizenship by Investment Programme
routes.summary: Government-administered direct citizenship programme with contribution, real-estate and other approved investment options.

sources.title: Antigua and Barbuda Citizenship by Investment Unit

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in Australia
routes.summary: A child born in Australia is a citizen when a parent is a citizen or permanent resident, or when the child is ordinarily resident in Australia for the first ten years of life.

pathways.label: Citizen or permanent-resident parent at birth
First ten years ordinarily resident in Australia

sources.title: Australian Citizenship Act 2007

routes.title: Citizenship by conferral after residence
routes.summary: The general residence requirement is four years on a valid visa, including the final 12 months as a permanent resident or eligible Special Category Visa holder.

pathways.label: General residence route
pathways.note: Absence, character, test, and pledge requirements also apply.

sources.title: Australian Home Affairs — Citizenship residence calculator
Australian Citizenship Act 2007

routes.title: Citizenship by descent
routes.summary: A person born outside Australia may apply by descent when a parent was an Australian citizen at the time of birth.

pathways.label: Australian citizen parent at birth
pathways.note: If the parent was themselves a citizen by descent, a two-year lawful-presence condition can apply.

sources.title: Australian Home Affairs — Citizenship by descent
Australian Citizenship Act 2007

routes.title: Naturalization for a person of Bulgarian origin
routes.summary: A person of Bulgarian origin may apply under article 15 without the ordinary residence, income, language and release-of-prior-citizenship conditions. The applicant must establish the family relationship with at least one ancestor of Bulgarian origin, up to the third degree inclusive, using the required official documents.

pathways.label: Naturalization for a person of Bulgarian origin

sources.title: Bulgarian Ministry of Justice — Citizenship Act

routes.title: Citizenship by birth where no other citizenship is acquired
routes.summary: A person born in Bulgaria is a citizen by place of birth if they do not acquire another citizenship by origin; a foundling with unknown parents is presumed born in Bulgaria.

pathways.label: Citizenship by birth where no other citizenship is acquired

sources.title: Bulgarian Ministry of Justice — Citizenship Act

routes.title: Former investor-citizenship provisions
routes.summary: Bulgaria repealed the special investment-based citizenship provisions in 2022. Investment or property ownership should not be presented as a current direct route to Bulgarian citizenship; any residence obtained on another legal basis follows the applicable ordinary nationality rules.

pathways.label: Former investor-citizenship provisions
pathways.note: Historical negative retained to prevent stale investor-citizenship recommendations.

sources.title: Bulgarian National Assembly — investment citizenship repealed
Bulgarian Ministry of Justice — Citizenship Act

routes.title: Ordinary naturalization after permanent residence
routes.summary: Bulgaria's ordinary route generally requires an adult to have held permanent or long-term residence for at least five years, alongside criminal-record, income or occupation, Bulgarian-language and—unless an exception applies—prior-citizenship release requirements. This is five years after the qualifying residence status, not a promise of citizenship after five years of any lawful stay.

pathways.label: Ordinary naturalization after permanent residence
pathways.note: The five years run from obtaining permanent or long-term residence, not merely from first arrival.

sources.title: Bulgarian Ministry of Justice — Citizenship Act
Bulgarian Ministry of Justice — Citizenship Directorate

routes.title: Investor and donor naturalization exists in law; current operation needs verification
routes.summary: Cambodia's nationality law contains investment and donation provisions, but a current government-administered programme and operational requirements have not been verified for live recommendations.

sources.title: Cambodia Committee for Investment and Development — Law on Nationality

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in Canada
routes.summary: A person born in Canada is generally a citizen at birth, with a narrow exception for children of certain foreign diplomatic personnel.

sources.title: IRCC — Check if you may be a Canadian citizen

routes.title: Citizenship through a Canadian parent
routes.summary: A child born abroad to a Canadian parent is generally a citizen; from 15 December 2025, a Canadian parent also born abroad must usually have spent 1,095 days in Canada before the child’s birth.

pathways.label: Canadian parent born or naturalized in Canada
Canadian parent also born abroad
pathways.note: This substantial-connection test applies to births on or after 15 December 2025.

sources.title: IRCC — Check if you may be a Canadian citizen

routes.title: Citizenship grant after physical presence
routes.summary: An adult permanent resident generally needs 1,095 days of physical presence in the five-year eligibility period, including at least 730 days as a permanent resident.

pathways.label: Standard adult grant
pathways.note: Tax, language, test, oath, and prohibition rules can also apply.

sources.title: IRCC — Canadian citizenship eligibility

routes.title: Visitor student visa time does not accumulate toward permanent residence
routes.summary: Colombia's current student route is a Visitor (V) visa. The Foreign Ministry states that study time does not count toward a Resident (R) visa by accumulated time; article 90 lists qualifying Migrant (M) categories and does not include the V student visa.

sources.title: Colombia Foreign Ministry — Visa FAQ
Resolution 5477 of 2022, article 90 (as amended)
Colombia Foreign Ministry — V Student Visa

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Cypriot parent
routes.summary: A person born abroad after 16 August 1960 can use the consular-birth route where a mother or father was a Cypriot citizen at the time of birth, subject to statutory exceptions and registration.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a Cypriot parent

sources.title: Cyprus Ministry of Interior — Citizenship due to Cypriot origin

routes.title: Cypriot citizenship due to origin
routes.summary: Cypriot-origin registration routes cover people born abroad and specified descendants, using the applicable M121, M123, M124 or M126 procedure.

sources.title: Cyprus Ministry of Interior — Citizenship due to Cypriot origin

routes.title: Cypriot naturalization by residence
routes.summary: The ordinary route requires twelve continuous months immediately before application and at least seven cumulative lawful years in the preceding ten years, plus language, civic, character and resources tests.

sources.title: Cyprus Ministry of Interior — Naturalization due to residence

routes.title: Citizenship by Investment Programme
routes.summary: Government-administered direct citizenship programme offering a public-fund contribution and approved real-estate route.

sources.title: Dominica Citizenship by Investment Unit

routes.title: Citizenship through birth and residence in France
routes.summary: Birth in France alone is not generally enough. Limited parentage or statelessness cases confer citizenship at birth; otherwise a child of foreign parents can acquire it from age 13, 16, or automatically at 18 when the residence conditions are met.

pathways.label: Born in France to a parent also born in France
pathways.note: Citizenship from birth under the double-jus-soli rule, subject to the diplomatic exception.

pathways.label: Stateless parents or no nationality transmitted
pathways.note: Citizenship from birth where the child would otherwise receive no nationality from either parent.

pathways.label: Parent declaration from age 13
pathways.note: From age 13, a parent may declare with the child’s consent after at least five years of habitual residence in France since age 8.

pathways.label: Personal declaration from age 16
pathways.note: At age 16 or 17, the child may declare while living in France after at least five years of habitual residence since age 11.

pathways.label: Automatic acquisition at age 18
pathways.note: Automatic at age 18 if the person lives in France then and has accumulated at least five years of habitual residence since age 11.

sources.title: French Civil Code — Article 19-3
French Civil Code — Article 20-5
French Civil Code — Article 19-1
French Civil Code — Article 21-11
Service-Public — French nationality for a child born in France
French Civil Code — Article 21-7

routes.title: Citizenship through a French parent
routes.summary: A child is French when at least one parent is French.

pathways.label: At least one French parent
pathways.note: The entitlement is by filiation; documentation and registration time are separate.

sources.title: French Civil Code — Article 18

routes.title: Naturalization after residence
routes.summary: French Civil Code article 21-17 ordinarily requires five years of habitual residence and does not exclude lawful student residence. Article 21-18 reduces that period to two years for a person who successfully completes two years of higher education toward a diploma from a French university or higher-education institution. A valid permit, continuing residence, assimilation, language and stable professional integration remain material; naturalization is discretionary.

facts.reduction_condition: Successfully complete two years of higher education toward a diploma from a French university or higher-education institution

pathways.label: Ordinary residence
pathways.note: Eligibility period only; naturalization remains discretionary.

pathways.label: French higher-education reduction
pathways.note: Reduced eligibility period, not a processing-time promise.

sources.title: French Civil Code, article 21-17
Service-Public — Naturalisation française par décret
French Civil Code, article 21-18
CESEDA — student residence permits

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in Germany
routes.summary: A child of foreign parents born in Germany acquires citizenship when one parent meets the residence and permanent-status conditions.

pathways.label: Parent resident for five years with permanent status
pathways.note: The five-year period is a condition already met by the parent before birth.

sources.title: German Nationality Act (Staatsangehörigkeitsgesetz)

routes.title: Citizenship through a German parent
routes.summary: A child generally acquires German citizenship at birth when at least one parent is German.

pathways.label: German parent at birth
pathways.note: Citizenship is acquired at birth when the statutory conditions are met.

sources.title: German Nationality Act (Staatsangehörigkeitsgesetz)

routes.title: Naturalization after residence
routes.summary: The standard entitlement uses five years of lawful ordinary residence, with a shorter rule for spouses of Germans.

pathways.label: Standard five-year route
pathways.note: Other requirements in section 10 still apply.

pathways.label: Spouse or registered partner of a German
pathways.note: The residence and relationship requirements operate together.

sources.title: German Nationality Act (Staatsangehörigkeitsgesetz)

routes.title: Citizenship following birth and schooling in Greece
routes.summary: A child born in Greece to foreign parents can acquire citizenship by declaration where the statutory parent-residence, lawful-status and Greek-school conditions are met.

pathways.label: Citizenship following birth and schooling in Greece
pathways.note: This is a declaration route connected to birth and schooling, not unconditional jus soli.

sources.title: Greek Ministry of Interior — Citizenship Code

routes.title: Citizenship routes through a Greek parent
routes.summary: Greek nationality law provides acquisition and declaration procedures for people born to a Greek parent, with separate documentary routes depending on birth date, registration and the parents' marital or acknowledgment status. This record confirms the route family but does not collapse those distinct procedures into one universal checklist.

pathways.label: Citizenship routes through a Greek parent

sources.title: Greek Ministry of Interior — Citizenship supporting documents

routes.title: Ordinary naturalization after residence
routes.summary: Greece's ordinary naturalization route for non-co-ethnic foreign nationals generally uses seven years of legal residence, with distinct eligible residence categories and supporting documents. Applicants must also satisfy the applicable integration, language, civic-knowledge and character requirements; residence eligibility is not an automatic grant.

sources.title: Greek Ministry of Interior — Citizenship supporting documents
Greek Ministry of Interior — seven-year naturalization period

routes.title: Citizenship by Investment Programme
routes.summary: Government-administered direct citizenship programme with National Transformation Fund and approved-project routes.

sources.title: Grenada Citizenship by Investment

routes.title: Citizenship by birth on the island of Ireland
routes.summary: For births from 2005, citizenship depends on a parent’s citizenship, entitlement, or qualifying residence before the birth.

pathways.label: Parent resident for three of the previous four years
pathways.note: Specified study and protection-processing periods do not count toward the parent’s residence calculation.

sources.title: Irish Nationality and Citizenship Act 2004 — Section 4

routes.title: Citizenship through an Irish parent or grandparent
routes.summary: Citizenship may be automatic through an Irish-born parent or available through Foreign Birth Registration in specified overseas cases.

pathways.label: Parent born on the island of Ireland
pathways.note: The Department of Foreign Affairs describes this category as automatic.

pathways.label: Foreign Birth Registration
pathways.note: Citizenship begins when the person is entered on the Foreign Births Register.

sources.title: Irish Department of Foreign Affairs — Born abroad

routes.title: Naturalization after reckonable residence
routes.summary: The standard adult route requires five years of reckonable residence in the previous nine, including one continuous year immediately before applying.

pathways.label: Standard adult naturalization
pathways.note: The Minister retains discretion and other statutory conditions apply.

sources.title: Irish Immigration Service — How to become an Irish citizen

routes.title: Citizenship through an Italian parent
routes.summary: A child of an Italian citizen can acquire citizenship by descent, but the 2025 reform limits automatic transmission to foreign-born people who hold another citizenship unless a statutory connection exception applies.

sources.title: Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs — Citizenship

routes.title: Limited citizenship routes connected to birth in Italy
routes.summary: Birth in Italy is not generally enough. Citizenship exists at birth in limited statelessness or non-transmission cases, while a foreign child born and continuously resident in Italy may declare citizenship after reaching adulthood.

pathways.label: Born and continuously resident until adulthood
pathways.note: This is later acquisition because of birth and residence, not unconditional citizenship at birth.

sources.title: Italian Ministry of the Interior — Citizenship
Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs — Citizenship

routes.title: Naturalization after legal residence
routes.summary: The general route permits an application after ten years of legal residence, with shorter periods for specified groups.

pathways.label: General ten-year route
pathways.note: Other statutory requirements and differentiated residence periods can apply.

sources.title: Italian Ministry of the Interior — Citizenship
Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs — Citizenship

routes.title: Investor citizenship criteria
routes.summary: Jordan's Cabinet-approved criteria provide citizenship routes through qualifying securities, productive projects, business expansion or sustained job creation; several project routes require a three-year compliance period.

sources.title: Jordanian Prime Ministry — Cabinet decisions of 15 July 2026

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a qualifying parent
routes.summary: For births on or after 1 August 1989, birth in Malta confers citizenship only where a parent has the qualifying citizenship or status required by the Act.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a qualifying parent

sources.title: Community Malta Agency — Acquisition of Citizenship

routes.title: Maltese citizenship by descent
routes.summary: Malta provides registration routes for specified family situations, including descendants in the direct line where the relevant line includes two consecutive ascendants born in Malta, and for certain second or subsequent generations born abroad. A foreign spouse of a Maltese citizen may also qualify for registration after five years of marriage, subject to the applicable conditions.

sources.title: Community Malta Agency — Acquisition of Citizenship

routes.title: Maltese naturalization by residence
routes.summary: An adult may apply after residing in Malta throughout the twelve months immediately before the application and for an aggregate of at least four years during the preceding six years. The applicant must also meet character and language requirements. Naturalization remains a ministerial, discretionary decision rather than an entitlement after five calendar years.

pathways.label: Maltese naturalization by residence
pathways.note: The statutory residence threshold establishes eligibility to apply, not an entitlement to citizenship.

sources.title: Community Malta Agency — Acquisition of Citizenship

routes.title: Former investor-citizenship programme
routes.summary: Malta discontinued the Granting of Citizenship for Exceptional Services programme following the Court of Justice of the European Union judgment. Current exceptional-merit naturalization is a discretionary route based on genuine exceptional service or contribution and residence; it should not be represented as a direct citizenship-by-investment product.

pathways.label: Former investor-citizenship programme
pathways.note: Historical route retained to state explicitly that transactional investor citizenship ended in 2025.

sources.title: Government of Malta — citizenship programme discontinued
Community Malta Agency — amendments to the Citizenship Act
Community Malta Agency — naturalisation on the basis of merit

routes.title: Economic and Climate Resilience Citizenship Program
routes.summary: A statutory contribution programme administered by the Nauru Program Office to fund economic development and climate resilience.

sources.title: Government of Nauru — programme launch
Nauru Economic and Climate Resilience Citizenship Act 2024

routes.title: Dutch citizenship through a parent
routes.summary: A child generally acquires Dutch citizenship automatically when the statutory Dutch-parent conditions are met at birth.

sources.title: IND — Dutch citizen by birth, acknowledgment or adoption
Netherlands Nationality Act

routes.title: Naturalization after legal residence
routes.summary: The standard naturalization route generally requires five consecutive years of lawful residence with a qualifying status.

pathways.label: Standard five-year route
pathways.note: Status, integration, public-order, and usually renunciation requirements also apply; statutory exceptions can shorten the term.

sources.title: IND — Dutch citizenship through naturalisation
Netherlands Nationality Act

routes.title: Automatic citizenship for a qualifying third generation
routes.summary: A child can acquire Dutch citizenship at birth under the statutory third-generation principal-residence rule even without a Dutch parent.

sources.title: IND — Dutch citizen by birth, acknowledgment or adoption
Netherlands Nationality Act

routes.title: Investor and contribution citizenship programmes
routes.summary: The Citizenship Commission lists investor citizenship, the Development Support Program, the Vanuatu Contribution Program and a real-estate option under the Citizenship Act framework.

sources.title: Vanuatu Citizenship Commission — Types of Citizenship
Vanuatu Citizenship Commission — Legislative Framework

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in New Zealand
routes.summary: For births from 1 January 2006, a child born in New Zealand is a citizen when at least one parent is a citizen or has the right to live in New Zealand indefinitely.

sources.title: New Zealand Government — Citizenship by birth, descent and grant

routes.title: Citizenship by descent
routes.summary: A person born overseas can register as a citizen by descent when a parent was a New Zealand citizen by birth or grant at the time of birth.

pathways.label: Parent citizen by birth or grant
pathways.note: A citizen by descent generally cannot transmit citizenship to another overseas-born generation.

sources.title: New Zealand Government — Citizenship by birth, descent and grant

routes.title: Citizenship by grant after residence
routes.summary: Most adults need five years as a resident, 1,350 days of physical presence in total, and at least 240 days in each of those five years.

pathways.label: Standard five-year presence route
pathways.note: Language, character, and intention-to-reside requirements also apply.

sources.title: New Zealand Government — Citizenship presence requirements
New Zealand Government — Citizenship by birth, descent and grant

routes.title: Conditional citizenship through birth in Portugal
routes.summary: The 2026 nationality-law changes provide a birth-in-Portugal route where, at the time of birth, one parent has legally resided in Portugal for at least five years. This is a conditional jus soli rule, not unconditional citizenship for every child born in Portugal; additional statutory conditions and implementation rules must be checked for an individual case.

pathways.label: Parent with five years of legal residence
pathways.note: The five-year period applies to the parent before the child’s birth.

sources.title: Portuguese Ministry of Justice — Nationality Law: new rules effective 19 May

routes.title: Citizenship through a Portuguese parent
routes.summary: A person born abroad can obtain Portuguese nationality when a parent was Portuguese at the time of birth.

pathways.label: Portuguese parent at birth
pathways.note: Eligibility is by descent; registration and processing time are separate.

sources.title: Portuguese Ministry of Justice — Portuguese nationality
Portuguese Ministry of Justice — Submit a nationality application

routes.title: Ordinary naturalization after legal residence
routes.summary: For applications made from 19 May 2026, the ordinary legal-residence period is seven years for nationals of Portuguese-speaking countries and EU Member States, and ten years for other nationals. Applications already pending when the law entered into force remain under the earlier rules. The Ministry of Justice notes that some practical implementation still requires supplementary regulation; eligibility is not an automatic grant.

pathways.label: CPLP or EU national
pathways.note: Eligibility period for applications under the 2026 law.

pathways.label: Other national
pathways.note: General eligibility period for applications under the 2026 law.

sources.title: Portuguese Ministry of Justice — Nationality Law: new rules effective 19 May
Portuguese Ministry of Justice — Portuguese nationality

routes.title: Citizenship Programme
routes.summary: Government-administered direct citizenship programme requiring a prescribed investment or contribution and due diligence.

sources.title: St. Kitts and Nevis Citizenship Unit

routes.title: Citizenship by Investment Programme
routes.summary: Direct citizenship by registration following a qualifying investment under the Citizenship by Investment Act.

sources.title: Saint Lucia Citizenship by Investment Act

routes.title: Citizenship by Investment Programme
routes.summary: A direct contribution route created under Decree-Law 07/2025 and promoted through the country's official consular network.

sources.title: Honorary Consulate of São Tomé and Príncipe — Citizenship by Investment

routes.title: Admission after permanent residence
routes.summary: An adult foreigner with permanent residence may request admission to Serbian citizenship subject to release or qualifying assurance regarding former citizenship and a declaration that Serbia is their state.

pathways.label: Admission after permanent residence
pathways.note: Current foreigner rules generally make permanent residence available after three years of continuous temporary residence; citizenship admission then remains a separate decision.

sources.title: Serbian Ministry of Interior — Citizenship

routes.title: Citizenship at birth to prevent statelessness
routes.summary: A child born or found in Serbia acquires citizenship where both parents are unknown, of unknown citizenship or stateless, or where the child would otherwise be stateless.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth to prevent statelessness

sources.title: Serbian Ministry of Interior — Citizenship

routes.title: Serbian citizenship by descent
routes.summary: Serbian citizenship is acquired by descent under the parentage and, for some foreign-born children, registration conditions in the Citizenship Act.

sources.title: Serbian Ministry of Interior — Citizenship

routes.title: Citizenship application after permanent residence
routes.summary: An adult permanent resident is eligible to apply after at least two years as a PR; approval remains discretionary.

pathways.label: Adult permanent resident
pathways.note: Eligibility to apply is not an entitlement to approval; national-service and other rules can apply.

sources.title: Singapore ICA — Becoming a Singapore citizen
Constitution of the Republic of Singapore — Citizenship

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in Singapore
routes.summary: A person born in Singapore is a citizen by birth when at least one parent is a Singapore citizen, subject to the constitutional exceptions.

pathways.label: Born in Singapore to a citizen parent

sources.title: Constitution of the Republic of Singapore — Citizenship

routes.title: Citizenship by descent
routes.summary: A person born outside Singapore can acquire citizenship by descent through a Singapore citizen parent, subject to registration and additional connection rules where the parent is also a citizen by descent.

pathways.label: Singapore citizen parent
pathways.note: A citizen-by-descent parent must also satisfy the Constitution’s Singapore-residence connection test.

sources.title: Constitution of the Republic of Singapore — Citizenship
Singapore ICA — Becoming a Singapore citizen

routes.title: Citizenship through birth in Spain
routes.summary: Birth in Spain alone does not generally confer citizenship. Nationality by origin applies in limited parentage, statelessness, or undetermined-parentage cases.

pathways.label: Foreign parent also born in Spain
No nationality transmitted by either parent
Undetermined parentage
pathways.note: Spanish origin nationality applies when parentage is undetermined; the Civil Code also presumes certain found minors were born in Spain.

sources.title: Spanish Civil Code — Articles 17, 20 and 22

routes.title: Citizenship through a Spanish parent or option
routes.summary: Spain recognizes nationality by origin through a Spanish parent and an option route for some children of originally Spanish parents.

pathways.label: Spanish parent
pathways.note: Nationality by origin; documentation time is separate.

pathways.label: Option through an originally Spanish parent born in Spain
pathways.note: A statutory option route under Civil Code Article 20.

sources.title: Spanish Civil Code — Articles 17, 20 and 22

routes.title: Naturalization after legal residence
routes.summary: The general residence period is ten years and five years for recognized refugees. A person born in Spain who is not already Spanish by origin can apply after one year of legal, continuous residence immediately before applying; the grant is not automatic.

pathways.label: Ordinary residence
pathways.note: Eligibility period only; the grant is not automatic.

pathways.label: Recognized refugee
pathways.note: Five-year residence period for a person recognized as a refugee.

pathways.label: Born in Spain
pathways.note: One year of legal, continuous residence immediately before the application. Article 22 also requires good civic conduct and sufficient integration; the grant is not automatic.

sources.title: Spanish Civil Code — Articles 17, 20 and 22

routes.title: Swiss citizenship through a parent
routes.summary: Swiss citizenship is principally acquired through paternal or maternal descent rather than birthplace alone.

sources.title: Swiss SEM — Acquiring Swiss citizenship

routes.title: Ordinary naturalization
routes.summary: Ordinary naturalization generally requires ten years in Switzerland and a permanent C permit, alongside federal, cantonal, and communal requirements.

pathways.label: Ten-year route with C permit
pathways.note: Three years must fall within the five years before filing; cantonal and communal residence rules also apply.

sources.title: Swiss SEM — Ordinary naturalisation

routes.title: Facilitated naturalization for a third generation born in Switzerland
routes.summary: Birth in Switzerland does not itself grant citizenship, but a qualifying third-generation applicant born there may use facilitated naturalization before age 25.

pathways.label: Third generation born in Switzerland
pathways.note: This is later facilitated naturalization connected to birthplace and family residence, not citizenship at birth.

sources.title: Swiss SEM — Third-generation naturalisation
Swiss SEM — Acquiring Swiss citizenship

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a qualifying parent
routes.summary: Citizenship at birth follows the federal parentage rules rather than territorial birth; the law also protects certain children of unknown or stateless parentage.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth through a qualifying parent

sources.title: UAE Ministry of Justice — Nationality and Passports Law

routes.title: Emirati nationality through a citizen father
routes.summary: Federal law treats a person born in the UAE or abroad to a qualifying Emirati father as a citizen by operation of law, with additional limited maternal and foundling cases.

pathways.label: Emirati nationality through a citizen father

sources.title: UAE Ministry of Justice — Nationality and Passports Law

routes.title: Exceptional nomination for Emirati nationality
routes.summary: Specified professionals, scientists, inventors, intellectuals and talented people may be nominated for nationality when their category-specific conditions are met.

pathways.label: Exceptional nomination for Emirati nationality
pathways.note: There is no public self-application route or guaranteed timeline.

sources.title: UAE Government — Emirati nationality

routes.title: Investor nomination for Emirati nationality
routes.summary: The 2021 exceptional categories allow a property-owning investor to be nominated for nationality by designated UAE authorities.

pathways.label: Investor nomination for Emirati nationality
pathways.note: This is not a purchasable or threshold-based CBI programme. Golden Visa investment residence is separate.

sources.title: UAE Government — Emirati nationality

routes.title: Citizenship at birth to prevent statelessness
routes.summary: A child born in Türkiye who cannot acquire any nationality from the parents becomes Turkish from birth; a foundling is presumed born in Türkiye.

pathways.label: Citizenship at birth to prevent statelessness

sources.title: Türkiye NVI — Acquisition of Turkish citizenship

routes.title: Turkish citizenship by descent
routes.summary: A child acquires Turkish citizenship through a Turkish mother or father, subject to the parentage rules in Citizenship Law No. 5901.

sources.title: Turkish Citizenship Law No. 5901

routes.title: Exceptional citizenship through qualifying investment
routes.summary: Foreign investors meeting a prescribed property, capital, deposit, securities, pension-fund or job-creation condition may acquire citizenship by presidential decision.

pathways.label: Exceptional citizenship through qualifying investment
pathways.note: The property variant is modeled here; official guidance also lists USD 500,000 alternatives and a 50-job option.

sources.title: Invest in Türkiye — Acquiring Property and Citizenship

routes.title: Turkish naturalization after residence
routes.summary: An adult can apply under the general provisions after five continuous years of residence and satisfaction of settlement, language, character, health and security conditions.

sources.title: Türkiye NVI — Acquisition of Turkish citizenship

routes.title: Egyptian citizenship through investment programmes
routes.summary: The Prime Minister's citizenship unit administers direct routes through a non-refundable contribution, property, an investment project or a central-bank deposit.

sources.title: Egyptian Cabinet — Citizenship through Investment Programmes
GAFI — Unit for Granting Egyptian Citizenship in Exchange for Investment

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in the UK
routes.summary: For births from 1983, a child is normally automatically British when a parent is British or settled at the time of birth.

sources.title: GOV.UK — British citizenship for people born in the UK

routes.title: Citizenship through a British parent
routes.summary: British citizenship can usually pass one generation to a child born outside the UK, subject to the parent’s status and the date and place of birth.

pathways.label: British parent
pathways.note: Some categories are automatic and others require registration.

sources.title: GOV.UK — British citizenship through a British parent

routes.title: Naturalization after settlement
routes.summary: The standard route combines five years of residence with at least 12 months holding indefinite leave, settled status, or indefinite leave to enter.

pathways.label: Standard settled route
pathways.note: The residence and settled-status periods overlap when settlement was obtained earlier.

pathways.label: Spouse or civil partner of a British citizen
pathways.note: No additional 12-month wait after settlement applies to this variant.

sources.title: GOV.UK — Naturalization with indefinite leave or settled status

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a U.S. citizen parent
routes.summary: A child born abroad can acquire citizenship at birth when the citizen parent meets the applicable residence, physical-presence, parentage, and date-of-birth rules.

pathways.label: One U.S. citizen parent for births from 14 November 1986
pathways.note: Different historical and family configurations use different statutory tests.

sources.title: U.S. Department of State — Citizenship at birth abroad

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in the United States
routes.summary: Federal statute grants citizenship at birth to a person born in the United States and subject to its jurisdiction.

pathways.label: Born in the United States and subject to its jurisdiction

sources.title: U.S. Code — 8 USC 1401

routes.title: Naturalization after permanent residence
routes.summary: The standard route requires five years as a lawful permanent resident and at least 30 months of physical presence in that period.

pathways.label: Five-year LPR route
pathways.note: Continuous residence, character, English, civics, and oath requirements also apply.

sources.title: USCIS — Naturalization for lawful permanent residents

routes.title: Uruguayan legal citizenship by habitual residence
routes.summary: A foreign adult of good conduct may seek legal citizenship after three years of habitual residence with family constituted in Uruguay, or five years without, while proving qualifying means or activity.

pathways.label: Uruguayan legal citizenship by habitual residence
pathways.note: Without family constituted in Uruguay the period is five years. An absence exceeding six continuous months resets the residence period.

sources.title: Uruguay Electoral Court — Legal citizenship application

routes.title: Uruguayan nationality by territorial birth
routes.summary: Every person born anywhere in the territory of Uruguay is a natural citizen and Uruguayan national.

sources.title: Uruguay Constitution — Article 74

routes.title: Uruguayan nationality through a parent
routes.summary: A child of a Uruguayan mother or father is a Uruguayan national regardless of birthplace; constitutional natural-citizenship exercise involves the applicable connection and registration steps.

sources.title: Uruguay Law No. 16.021 — Nationality

routes.title: Citizenship Investment Act exists; current programme operation needs verification
routes.summary: Samoa has a Citizenship Investment Act and was reviewing the programme framework in 2025, but current application operations and qualifying guidelines are not yet verified for live recommendations.

sources.title: Government of Samoa — Citizenship Investment Act 2015
Samoa Ministry of Commerce — 2025 policy framework workshop
